meta.title: Flex
meta.description: Flex is a layout component that provides a convenient way to create flexible layouts based on CSS Flexbox. It's based on the Chakra UI Flex component and supports all style props for rapid UI development.

mdx:
## Overview

The Flex component is essentially a Box component with `display: flex` applied
by default. It provides an intuitive API for creating flexible layouts and
supports all flexbox properties.

### Key features

- **Flexbox by default**: Automatically applies `display: flex`
- **Style props**: Supports all Chakra UI style props for rapid development
- **Responsive design**: Easily create responsive layouts with responsive props
- **Semantic HTML**: Can render as any HTML element using the `as` prop

### Resources

Learn more about CSS Flexbox and the underlying implementation:

[Chakra UI Flex](https://chakra-ui.com/docs/components/flex)
[CSS Flexbox Guide](https://css-tricks.com/snippets/css/a-guide-to-flexbox/)

## Usage

### Import

```jsx
import { Flex } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
```

### Basic usage

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex gap="400">
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 2
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 3
    </Box>
  </Flex>
);
```

### Direction

Control the direction of flex items using the `direction` prop:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="600">
    <Stack gap="200">
      <Text fontWeight="500">Horizontal (row):</Text>
      <Flex direction="row" gap="400">
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 1
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 2
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 3
        </Box>
      </Flex>
    </Stack>
    <Stack gap="200">
      <Text fontWeight="500">Vertical (column):</Text>
      <Flex direction="column" gap="400">
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 1
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 2
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 3
        </Box>
      </Flex>
    </Stack>
  </Stack>
);
```

### Alignment

Control alignment of flex items using `alignItems` and `justifyContent`:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex
    direction="row"
    gap="400"
    alignItems="center"
    justifyContent="space-between"
    p="400"
    bg="neutral.2"
  >
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 2
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 3
    </Box>
  </Flex>
);
```

### Wrapping

Control how flex items wrap using the `wrap` prop:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex wrap="wrap" gap="400" maxWidth="600px">
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" minWidth="150px">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" minWidth="150px">
      Item 2
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" minWidth="150px">
      Item 3
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" minWidth="150px">
      Item 4
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" minWidth="150px">
      Item 5
    </Box>
  </Flex>
);
```

### Flexible items

Control how individual items grow and shrink using flex properties:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex gap="400">
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" flex="1">
      Flexible item (flex: 1)
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="positive.7">
      Fixed width item
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" flex="2">
      More flexible item (flex: 2)
    </Box>
  </Flex>
);
```

### Centering content

A common use case is centering content both horizontally and vertically:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex
    alignItems="center"
    justifyContent="center"
    height="200px"
    bg="neutral.2"
  >
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Centered content
    </Box>
  </Flex>
);
```

### With asChild

Use the `asChild` prop to apply Flex styles to any child element while
maintaining the child's functionality:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex gap="400" p="400" bg="neutral.2" asChild>
    <section>
      <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
        Item 1
      </Box>
      <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
        Item 2
      </Box>
    </section>
  </Flex>
);
```

overview.mdx:
## Overview

The Flex component is essentially a Box component with `display: flex` applied
by default. It provides an intuitive API for creating flexible layouts and
supports all flexbox properties.

### Key features

- **Flexbox by default**: Automatically applies `display: flex`
- **Style props**: Supports all Chakra UI style props for rapid development
- **Responsive design**: Easily create responsive layouts with responsive props
- **Semantic HTML**: Can render as any HTML element using the `as` prop

### Resources

Learn more about CSS Flexbox and the underlying implementation:

[Chakra UI Flex](https://chakra-ui.com/docs/components/flex)
[CSS Flexbox Guide](https://css-tricks.com/snippets/css/a-guide-to-flexbox/)

## Usage

### Import

```jsx
import { Flex } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
```

### Basic usage

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex gap="400">
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 2
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 3
    </Box>
  </Flex>
);
```

### Direction

Control the direction of flex items using the `direction` prop:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="600">
    <Stack gap="200">
      <Text fontWeight="500">Horizontal (row):</Text>
      <Flex direction="row" gap="400">
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 1
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 2
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 3
        </Box>
      </Flex>
    </Stack>
    <Stack gap="200">
      <Text fontWeight="500">Vertical (column):</Text>
      <Flex direction="column" gap="400">
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 1
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 2
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
          Item 3
        </Box>
      </Flex>
    </Stack>
  </Stack>
);
```

### Alignment

Control alignment of flex items using `alignItems` and `justifyContent`:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex
    direction="row"
    gap="400"
    alignItems="center"
    justifyContent="space-between"
    p="400"
    bg="neutral.2"
  >
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 2
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 3
    </Box>
  </Flex>
);
```

### Wrapping

Control how flex items wrap using the `wrap` prop:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex wrap="wrap" gap="400" maxWidth="600px">
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" minWidth="150px">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" minWidth="150px">
      Item 2
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" minWidth="150px">
      Item 3
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" minWidth="150px">
      Item 4
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" minWidth="150px">
      Item 5
    </Box>
  </Flex>
);
```

### Flexible items

Control how individual items grow and shrink using flex properties:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex gap="400">
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" flex="1">
      Flexible item (flex: 1)
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="positive.7">
      Fixed width item
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" flex="2">
      More flexible item (flex: 2)
    </Box>
  </Flex>
);
```

### Centering content

A common use case is centering content both horizontally and vertically:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex
    alignItems="center"
    justifyContent="center"
    height="200px"
    bg="neutral.2"
  >
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Centered content
    </Box>
  </Flex>
);
```

### With asChild

Use the `asChild` prop to apply Flex styles to any child element while
maintaining the child's functionality:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex gap="400" p="400" bg="neutral.2" asChild>
    <section>
      <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
        Item 1
      </Box>
      <Box p="400" bg="primary.7">
        Item 2
      </Box>
    </section>
  </Flex>
);
```

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Flex as="nav" gap="400" p="400">
    <Box as="a" href="#" p="200">
      Home
    </Box>
    <Box as="a" href="#" p="200">
      About
    </Box>
    <Box as="a" href="#" p="200">
      Contact
    </Box>
  </Flex>
);
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Use semantic HTML**: Choose appropriate HTML elements with the `as` prop
- **Maintain reading order**: Ensure visual order matches DOM order for screen
  readers
- **Avoid layout-only divs**: When possible, apply flex styles to semantic
  elements

dev.mdx:
## Getting started

### Import

```tsx
import { Flex, type FlexProps } from '@commercetools/nimbus';
```

### Basic usage

The Flex component applies `display: flex` to create flexible layouts. By default, items are arranged horizontally in a single row:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Flex gap="400">
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200">
      Item 2
    </Box>
    <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200">
      Item 3
    </Box>
  </Flex>
)
```

## Usage examples

### Direction control

Use the `direction` prop to arrange items horizontally or vertically:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="600">
    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">Row (horizontal):</Text>
      <Flex direction="row" gap="400" bg="neutral.3" p="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200">
          Item 1
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200">
          Item 2
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200">
          Item 3
        </Box>
      </Flex>
    </Box>

    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">Column (vertical):</Text>
      <Flex direction="column" gap="400" bg="neutral.3" p="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Box p="400" bg="info.7" borderRadius="200">
          Item 1
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="info.7" borderRadius="200">
          Item 2
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="info.7" borderRadius="200">
          Item 3
        </Box>
      </Flex>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Alignment control

Use `alignItems` and `justifyContent` to control how items are positioned:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="600">
    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">Align items to center:</Text>
      <Flex direction="row" gap="400" alignItems="center" bg="neutral.3" p="400" borderRadius="200" height="1600">
        <Box p="200" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200">Short</Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200">
          <Text>Taller</Text>
          <Text>Content</Text>
        </Box>
        <Box p="200" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200">Short</Box>
      </Flex>
    </Box>

    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">Justify content with space-between:</Text>
      <Flex direction="row" gap="400" justifyContent="space-between" bg="neutral.3" p="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Box p="400" bg="info.7" borderRadius="200">Start</Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="info.7" borderRadius="200">Middle</Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="info.7" borderRadius="200">End</Box>
      </Flex>
    </Box>

    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">Center both axes:</Text>
      <Flex direction="row" alignItems="center" justifyContent="center" bg="neutral.3" p="600" borderRadius="200" height="2000">
        <Box p="400" bg="positive.7" borderRadius="200">
          Centered
        </Box>
      </Flex>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Item flexibility

Control how items grow and shrink using flex properties:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">Items with different flex values:</Text>
      <Flex direction="row" gap="400" bg="neutral.3" p="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200" flex="1">
          Flex: 1
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200">
          Fixed width
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200" flex="2">
          Flex: 2
        </Box>
      </Flex>
    </Box>

    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">No grow (minWidth constraint):</Text>
      <Flex direction="row" gap="400" bg="neutral.3" p="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Box p="400" bg="info.7" borderRadius="200" minWidth="1200">
          Fixed min width
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="info.7" borderRadius="200" minWidth="1200">
          Fixed min width
        </Box>
        <Box p="400" bg="info.7" borderRadius="200" minWidth="1200">
          Fixed min width
        </Box>
      </Flex>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Wrapping behavior

Control how items wrap when they exceed available space:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="600">
    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">Wrap enabled:</Text>
      <Flex wrap="wrap" gap="400" bg="neutral.3" p="400" borderRadius="200">
        {Array.from({ length: 6 }, (_, i) => (
          <Box key={i} p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200" minWidth="2000">
            Item {i + 1}
          </Box>
        ))}
      </Flex>
    </Box>

    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">No wrap (overflow):</Text>
      <Flex wrap="nowrap" gap="400" bg="neutral.3" p="400" borderRadius="200" overflow="auto">
        {Array.from({ length: 6 }, (_, i) => (
          <Box key={i} p="400" bg="info.7" borderRadius="200" minWidth="2000">
            Item {i + 1}
          </Box>
        ))}
      </Flex>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Responsive layouts

Use responsive prop values to change layout based on viewport size:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Flex
    direction={{ base: "column", md: "row" }}
    gap={{ base: "300", md: "600" }}
    alignItems={{ base: "stretch", md: "center" }}
  >
    <Box p="600" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200" flex="1">
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">Card 1</Text>
      <Text fontSize="sm">Stacks vertically on mobile, horizontally on desktop</Text>
    </Box>
    <Box p="600" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200" flex="1">
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">Card 2</Text>
      <Text fontSize="sm">Resize the window to see the layout change</Text>
    </Box>
    <Box p="600" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200" flex="1">
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">Card 3</Text>
      <Text fontSize="sm">Gap also adjusts responsively</Text>
    </Box>
  </Flex>
)
```

## Component requirements

### Usage context

Flex should be used when you need to:
- Arrange items in one direction (row or column) with flexible sizing
- Create dynamic layouts where items grow or shrink based on available space
- Build responsive navigation bars, button groups, or form layouts
- Align items with precise control over spacing and distribution

For two-dimensional grid layouts, consider using the `Grid` component. For simple vertical or horizontal stacking with consistent spacing, consider using `Stack` for a more semantic API.

## Accessibility

Flex is a layout component and does not have inherent accessibility requirements. However:

- Ensure child elements have appropriate accessibility attributes
- Use semantic HTML elements within Flex when appropriate
- Consider reading order when using `row-reverse` or `column-reverse` directions
- Test keyboard navigation flows through flex-arranged elements

If your use case requires tracking and analytics for this component, it is good practice to add a **persistent**, **unique** id to the component:

```tsx
const PERSISTENT_ID = "example-flex";

export const Example = () => (
  <Flex id={PERSISTENT_ID} direction="row" gap="400">
    {/* children */}
  </Flex>
);
```

#### Keyboard navigation

Flex is a non-interactive layout component and does not support keyboard navigation by default. Keyboard navigation is determined by the interactive child elements contained within the Flex.

## API reference

The component supports all [Style Props](/home/style-props) with `display="flex"` applied by default.

## Common patterns

### Centered content

Create a container that centers content both horizontally and vertically:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Flex
    direction="column"
    alignItems="center"
    justifyContent="center"
    height="3000"
    gap="400"
    bg="neutral.2"
    borderRadius="200"
  >
    <Box p="600" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="200" textAlign="center">
      <Text fontWeight="600" fontSize="lg" mb="200">Centered Content</Text>
      <Text fontSize="sm">This content is centered on both axes</Text>
    </Box>
  </Flex>
)
```

### Space-between layout

Distribute items with space between them:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Flex
    direction="row"
    justifyContent="space-between"
    alignItems="center"
    gap="400"
    p="400"
    bg="neutral.2"
    borderRadius="200"
  >
    <Text fontWeight="600">Logo</Text>
    <Flex direction="row" gap="200">
      <Button size="xs" variant="ghost">Home</Button>
      <Button size="xs" variant="ghost">About</Button>
      <Button size="xs" variant="outline">Sign In</Button>
    </Flex>
  </Flex>
)
```

### Flexible vs fixed items

Mix flexible and fixed-width items:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="400" mb="100">Sidebar layout:</Text>
      <Flex direction="row" gap="200" bg="neutral.3" p="200" borderRadius="100" height="3200">
        <Box p="400" bg="info.7" borderRadius="200" width="2400" flexShrink="0">
          Sidebar
        </Box>
        <Flex direction="column" gap="300" flex="1" bg="primary.7" p="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text fontWeight="600">Main Content</Text>
          <Text fontSize="sm">This content grows to fill available space</Text>
        </Flex>
      </Flex>
    </Box>

    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="600" mb="200">Header with flexible content:</Text>
      <Flex direction="row" gap="400" alignItems="center" bg="neutral.3" p="300" borderRadius="200">
        <Box  p="300" bg="neutral.6" borderRadius="100" flexShrink="0">
          Logo
        </Box>
        <Flex direction="column" gap="100" flex="1">
          <Text fontWeight="600" fontSize="sm">Flexible section</Text>
          <Text fontSize="xs" color="neutral.11">Grows with available space</Text>
        </Flex>
        <Button size="xs" flexShrink="0">Action</Button>
      </Flex>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

## Resources

- [Box Component](/components/layout/box)
- [Stack Component](/components/layout/stack)

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

Use the Flex component strategically to create flexible and responsive layouts.

### Best practices

- **Use for flexible layouts**: Flex is ideal when you need items to grow,
  shrink, or align dynamically
- **Consider Stack first**: For simple vertical or horizontal stacking with
  consistent spacing, Stack may be simpler
- **Leverage responsive props**: Use responsive values for direction, alignment,
  and other properties
- **Combine with other layout components**: Flex works well with Box, Stack, and
  Grid for complex layouts

### When to use

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- **Dynamic layouts**: When items need to grow or shrink based on available
  space
- **Complex alignment**: When you need precise control over item alignment
- **Responsive designs**: When layout needs to change based on viewport size
- **Flexible spacing**: When items should distribute space dynamically

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- **Simple stacking**: Use Stack instead for basic vertical/horizontal layouts
  with consistent spacing
- **Grid layouts**: Use Grid for two-dimensional layouts with rows and columns
- **Static layouts**: Use Box when flexbox features aren't needed
